tier_patterns.name: tier1_task_only_pattern
tier_patterns.description: Minimal pattern for creative / exploratory agents where only the Task component is defined.

task_fields.name: objective
task_fields.description: One-sentence statement of what the agent must achieve.

task_fields.name: acceptance_criteria
task_fields.description: List of MUST / NEVER statements that define when an output is considered valid.

task_fields.name: output_contract
task_fields.description: Description of allowed output format (e.g. plain text, bullets) and explicit exclusions.

task_fields.name: rule_block_references
task_fields.description: Optional list of referenced rule blocks by stable name (no rule text).

tier_patterns.name: tier2_task_plus_minimal_plan_pattern
tier_patterns.description: Task plus light Execution Plan for agents needing some structure and validation but no heavy rule blocks.

task_fields.name: objective
task_fields.description: Clear description of what the agent must deliver for the user.

task_fields.name: acceptance_criteria
task_fields.description: MUST / NEVER constraints, including high-level validation expectations (e.g. MUST cite FAQ source).

task_fields.name: output_contract
task_fields.description: Format and scope contract (e.g. text answers with inline citations, no JSON).

task_fields.name: rule_block_references
task_fields.description: Optional references to one or two light rule blocks (e.g. citation format, tone rules).

tier_patterns.name: tier3_task_plan_rules_pattern
tier_patterns.description: Full orthogonal pattern with Task, Execution Plan, and Rule Blocks for deterministic, schema-bound agents.

task_fields.name: objective
task_fields.description: Single-sentence statement of the deterministic outcome (e.g. generate precise quote, produce valid JSON).

task_fields.name: acceptance_criteria
task_fields.description: Explicit MUST / NEVER constraints including schema compliance, guardrails, and rule-application requirements.

task_fields.name: output_contract
task_fields.description: Strict format contract (e.g. JSON only, HTML only, Python code block only) and scope of allowed content.

task_fields.name: rule_block_references
task_fields.description: List of required rule block names that govern syntax, schemas, mappings, guardrails, markup, and tone.

tier_patterns.name: tier3_brief_writing_agent_pattern
tier_patterns.description: Specialized Tier 3 pattern for brand-specific brief-writing with citations, guardrails, and markup.

task_fields.name: objective
task_fields.description: Generate a comprehensive, compliant creative brief for a specific account with correct citations and gap highlighting.

task_fields.name: acceptance_criteria
task_fields.description: MUST include citations for all Supporting Data references; MUST mark missing information with gap highlights; MUST follow context source mapping; MUST apply brand guardrails; MUST output valid HTML using the brief structure schema; MUST NEVER fabricate unknowns (use GAP/TBD).

task_fields.name: output_contract
task_fields.description: HTML-only output with a fixed brief structure (BRIEF / SCOPE sections and subsections) and allowed tags restricted to headings, paragraphs, lists, mark, sup, br, hr.

task_fields.name: rule_block_references
task_fields.description: References to citation_formatting_rules, context_source_mapping_rules, highlight_formatting_rules, brand_guardrail_rules, markup_system_rules, and brief_html_structure_rules.

tier_patterns_markdown_summary: Tier 1 templates define only Task (objective, acceptance criteria, output contract) and are suited for creative, low-risk agents. Tier 2 adds a minimal Execution Plan for light structure and validation but keeps rule blocks to a minimum. Tier 3 fully separates Task, Plan, and Rule Blocks and is used when deterministic formats, schemas, guardrails, and multi-step validation are required; the brief-writing agent is a canonical Tier 3 example.
